meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Ship Your First AI App
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Master project management for developers using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your first real-world AI project today.
content.answer_block: The secret to finishing projects isn't more code; it's granular scope management. By breaking your vision into 'Vertical Slices'—end-to-end features that actually work—you eliminate the 'feature creep' that kills momentum. Combine this with automated planning in Antigravity and iterative execution via Claude Code to turn ideas into deployed products.
content.intro:
You have a folder full of half-finished repositories and a mind brimming with app ideas that never see the light of day. It is the classic developer's paradox: the thrill of the blank canvas is quickly replaced by the paralyzing weight of an unmanageable roadmap. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a system.

Today, we pivot. We are moving away from aimless coding toward a disciplined, high-velocity development cycle. Using Antigravity to structure your logic and Claude Code to accelerate your implementation, you will learn to slice your projects into executable milestones. This is how you transition from a student writing scripts to an AI Builder shipping software.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Feature
steps.description: Identify the one core function that makes your app useful. We ignore the UI polish and focus on the engine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and break it down into the smallest possible functional 'vertical slice' that provides core value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Propose a 3-step architecture for a MVP centered on [Core Feature]'

steps.title: Automate the Project Roadmap
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a task tree that prevents you from getting lost in the weeds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a task list for the identified vertical slice, prioritizing tasks that prevent technical debt.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project_plan.md file with the prioritized task list from the analysis'

steps.title: Build the Data Contract
steps.description: Define your data structures before writing logic to ensure stability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define the schema and data flow for the core feature, ensuring it is scalable for future iterations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'touch schema.json && echo "Define the schema structure here" > schema.json'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write one module at a time, testing as you go.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the boilerplate code for the first module identified in our project plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code 'Implement the core logic for [Module Name] based on the established schema'

steps.title: Continuous Validation
steps.description: Review progress against your plan to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase against the original vertical slice goal and identify any unnecessary complexity.

pro_tips: Treat your README.md as a living project management tool; update it every time you finish a task.
Never build a secondary feature until the primary feature is deployed to a staging URL.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests before you write the functional code to lock in your requirements.
If a task takes longer than 4 hours, break it into two smaller, distinct sub-tasks.
Commit your code every time you reach a milestone, no matter how small, to build a visible history of progress.
content.next_steps: Once your first vertical slice is functional, deploy it to a platform like Vercel or Railway. Share the link in our community to get feedback and iterate toward your second feature.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting great ideas die in private repositories. Master the workflow that turns prototypes into production-ready software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
